LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE JUL-15-2022 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT DELTA DENTAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. MATTER IS OFF CALENDAR. ON JUNE 9, 2022, THE COURT ORDERED THIS MOTION AND DEMURRER OFF CALENDAR. MOVANT MUST COMPLY WITH CCP 1005(B) AND PROVIDE AT LEAST 16-COURT-DAYS NOTICE TO PROPERLY RE-SET THEM. MOVANT'S E-SERVICE ON JULY 5, 2022 TO RE-SET THEM IS INSUFFICIENT. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFF ADVISED MOVANT THAT HE WAS UNAVAILABLE BECAUSE HE WAS ON VACATION ON THE CALENDARED HEARING DATE. JUDGE: VEDICA PURI; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/VP)

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/jpt)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-10-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Plaintiff SYED NAZIM ALI (Tel: 408-372-1150) in pro per;
Atty. ANNIE LAU, Esq. of Fisher & Phillips, LLP (Tel: 415-490-9000) for defendant Delta Dental Insurance Company.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT DELTA DENTAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's demurrer to the amended complaint is overruled as to causes of action one through four.

The court liberally reads the amended complaint pursuant to CCP 452 and plaintiff pleads sufficient ultimate facts to support claims one through four. To state a claim for discrimination, a plaintiff must allege that (1) he is a member of a protected class; (2) he was qualified for the position he sought; (3) he suffered an adverse employment action; and (4) the adverse action occurred under circumstances suggesting a discriminatory motive. (Abed v. Western Dental Services, Inc. (2018) 23 Cal.App.5th 726, 736.)

Here, plaintiff meets those requirements. Plaintiff alleges that he is over 40 (age), Muslim (religion), and from Pakistan (race/national origin). (Amended Complaint ("AC"), pars. 21, 31.) Plaintiff alleges that he was qualified for the positions that he applied for. (AC, pars. 12-13, 15-16, 32.) Plaintiff suffered an adverse employment action. (AC, pars. 33.) Plaintiff alleges facts indicating invidious discrimination. (AC, pars. 22, 24, 25, 27, 28, 29, 30, 35, 39.)

Defendant's demurrer to causes of action five through eight are sustained without leave to amend. (CCP 430.10(e).) Whether defendant abused the USCIS H1B Visa Program when allegedly discriminating against plaintiff is part of plaintiff's FEHA discrimination claims (i.e., causes of action one through four). Causes of action five through eight are not severable from the FEHA claims. Causes of action five through eight are therefore superfluous and/or not cognizable.

Defendant's demurrer to cause of action nine is sustained without leave to amend. (CCP 430.10(e), (f).) The claim improperly conjoins the distinct claims of discrimination and retaliation in one claim. (See Reno v. Baird (1998) 18 Cal.4th 640, 645-646 [explaining the nature of a discrimination claim]; Yanowitz v. L'Oreal USA, Inc. (2005) 36 Cal.4th 1028, 1043 [setting forth elements of a retaliation claim].) To the extent that plaintiff is seeking to recover for discrimination, cause of action nine is superfluous. To the extent plaintiff is seeking to recover for retaliation, the cause of action fails as a matter of law because plaintiff fails to allege that he engaged in a protected activity. (See Lewis v. City of Benicia (2014) 224 Cal.App.4th 1519, 1533 [To establish a prima facie case of retaliation under the FEHA, Plaintiff must show: (1) he engaged in a protected activity; (2) his employer subjected him to an adverse employment action; and (3) a causal link exists between the protected activity and the employer's action].)

Defendant's argument that the amended complaint fails because allegations are based on information and belief founders. A "[p]laintiff may allege on information and belief any matters that are not within [her] personal knowledge, if [s]he has information leading [her] to believe that the allegations are true." (Pridonoff v. Balokovich (1951) 36 Cal.2d 788, 792.) Whether plaintiff can support his allegations is a matter for discovery.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-08-2022 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Plaintiff SYED "ALEX" NAZIM ALI (Tel: 951-267-1000) in pro per;
Atty. ANNIE LAU, Esq. of Fisher & Phillips, LLP (Tel: 415-490-9000) for defendant Delta Dental Insurance Company.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT DELTA DENTAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Delta Dental's demurrer is sustained with leave to amend.

Plaintiff's nearly 30-page opposition brief violates California Rules of Court, Rule 3.1113(d), which limits opening and responding memorandums to 15 pages. Plaintiff also filed 36 pages of unauthenticated exhibits, a supplemental opposition brief, and an additional 92 pages of unauthenticated exhibits. (See Evid. Code sec. 1400.) Plaintiff did not request judicial notice of these exhibits.

In any case, plaintiff's complaint fails to state facts sufficient to constitute his 12 causes of action. He is granted leave to amend the complaint one time to cure the defects identified by defendant.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/RBU)
